title: Implement SCHC compression in Python
description: Python implementation of RFC 8724 SCHC. Implement rules 0-4 from spec Appendix A. Compression and decompression. ACK-on-Error fragmentation. Generate test vectors for each rule.
notes: SCHC engine + fragmentation + whole-packet rules 0/1/3/4 + packet<->field extraction all done (schc/: codec,context,fragment,reassembly,headers,rules). Real IPv6+UDP+CoAP and RPL DIO/DAO packets compress/decompress end-to-end to exact bytes (03f). Only remaining vs 'rules 0-4': rule 2 (whole-packet ICMPv6 Echo) — the ICMPv6-only building block exists (ICMPV6_ECHO_RULE) but not yet a whole-packet IPv6+ICMPv6-echo profile. Variable-length CoAP token now handled (carried as tail).
close_reason: SCHC compression in Python COMPLETE: RFC 8724 engine (codec: BitWriter/BitReader, all CDA/MO incl mapping), rule context/selection, ACK-on-Error fragmentation+reassembly, and whole-packet rules 0-4 (link-local CoAP, global CoAP, ICMPv6 Echo, RPL DIO, RPL DAO) compressing/decompressing real IPv6+UDP+CoAP and ICMPv6/RPL packets to exact bytes (schc/ + ipv6/udp.py). Variable CoAP token/options handled as tail. Per-rule hand-computed test vectors live in tests/schc/; a standalone exported vector artifact for Rust/C is tracked under ijj/ajr.
